_source: Anchor data for the FS4 characterization and transport gates. Specific gravities (60/60) from the standard GPSA Engineering Data Book / Whitson & Brule Monograph 20 Appendix A compilation; normal boiling points come from the already-committed NIST table (nistVaporPressure.json). Dilute-gas viscosity anchors from the NIST WebBook reference correlations (methane: Lemmon & Jacobsen 2004; nitrogen: Lemmon & Jacobsen 2004) at 300 K, 1 atm.
_gate: alkaneRecovery: run Kesler-Lee/Lee-Kesler on a pure n-alkane's measured Tb + SG and require the predicted Tc/Pc/omega to land within the stated bands of the FS1 library values. The bands are honest correlation-accuracy bands (Kesler-Lee is calibrated on petroleum fractions, not pure components) set at ~1.5x observed error. diluteViscosity: LBC/Stiel-Thodos dilute-gas viscosity at 1 atm within relTol of the measured value.

soreideSmoke.note: nC6 (MW 86) sits below the Soreide C7+ calibration range; the ~6% high prediction documented here is the expected edge behavior, gated at 8% to catch transcription typos only

diluteViscosity.note: NIST WebBook, methane 300 K / 1 atm ~ 11.1 uPa-s
NIST WebBook, nitrogen 300 K / 1 atm ~ 17.9 uPa-s
